items.name: Help Defend the Astral Plane
items.description:
When you will start resting at the Wyrm Lookout Campsite an encounter will begin. You should get ready and adjust your party accordingly.

If you will want to completely loot the Astral Plane you will need one party member with very high strength (15 or above) to reach the chasms.

items.name: Boots of Uninhibited Kushigo
items.description: The best boots for most Monk Builds .

items.name: Enter the Circus of the Last Day
Evaluate the bond with your partner
The Lottery
items.description:
You can win quite a few items even without getting a jackpot, so it is recommended to right now just win the consolation prizes (do not steal the Djinni Ring ):

• Unlucky Thief's Gloves

• Reverse Rain Cloak

• Boots of Very Fast Blinking

• Staff of a Mumbling Wizard

• Comeback Handaxe

tooltip.lore: You can cast Misty Step, with the caveat that you will teleport leaving your clothes behind.
This loyal axe always returns to its owner after being thrown. However, it has no eyes and therefore dreadful aim, inevitably returning to its thrower blade-end-first.
The wearer is perpetually just a little bit damp.
Because it was designed by a compulsive mumbler, this staff can't distinguish between its spells, and so can only cast - Fireball or Firebolt at random.
Whenever the wearer steals anything, a piece of coal appears in their pocket, like a tiny, dusty condemnation.

items.name: Win the Jackpot
items.description: Steal the Djinni Ring from Akabi . Play the game and now this time win the Jackpot. You will be taken to the Jungle . Loot Band of the Mystic Scoundrel and Nyrulna before leaving.

items.name: Nyrulna
items.description: One of the best throwing and piercing weapons in the game.

tooltip.lore: The forgemaster Lirrico designed this weapon for the champion of the wind goddess Akadi. A key component in the forging was his home city's wind, and for a year after the weapon was made, no wind blew there at all.

items.name: Band of the Mystic Scoundrel
items.description: A core item of many spellswords builds to have the full utility of bonus action.

tooltip.lore: What better way to disguise one's transgressions than a quickly positioned phantasm after the deed is done?

items.name: Talk With Boney
items.description: She can make a statue of you for 5000 gold.

items.name: Talk With Dribbles the Clown
items.description: You can loot the Clown Hammer from him.

tooltip.lore: This hammer has a colourful history - it doesn't honk when you squeeze it, alas.

items.name: Find Dribbles the Clown
items.description:
You will need to find the following parts to complete the quest:

• Clown's Severed Hand

• Clown's Severed Torso

• Clown's Severed Pelvis

• Clown's Severed Foot

• Clown's Severed Leg

• Clown's Severed Arm

• Clown's Severed Head

After delivering all the parts you will receive Spellmight Gloves as a reward.

items.name: Spellmight Gloves
items.description: Excellent gloves for spells that use attack rolls, like Eldritch Blast ​ or Scorching Ray .

tooltip.lore: Designed for the jobbing spellcaster, these gloves are well-insulated to prevent digit immolation for the somatically inept.

items.name: The Pointy Hat
items.description: Can be stolen from a locked chest.

tooltip.lore: Sages over the course of ages have dealt very constructive and useful advice - however, none so sagacious as that of the wizard Otiluke, who once advised one of his students thusly: 'Never cast Speak with Dead on a loved one if there's unresolved tension there. It's never worth it. Trust me.'

items.name: Clown's Severed Hand
items.description: Can be stolen or acquired from Popper .

items.name: Dark Displacement Gloves
items.description: Sold by Popper .

tooltip.lore: Rudimentary teleportation sigils mark the palms of these gloves, like coiled snakes in a bath of pitch.

items.name: Hollow's Staff
items.description: Can be looted from Korilla .

tooltip.lore: This staff was wielded by Thibault Hollow, a supremely talented necromancer notable for his overwhelming fear of zombies. Many was the time when his dread lair would echo with a high-pitched shriek when one of them surprised him in a corridor, or when he was taking a bath.

items.name: Talk With Brothers Clements and Bill
Talk With Treesym Tara
items.description: Talk with Tara, bring Gale as well. She will give you Ring Of Blink .

tooltip.lore: Fashioned by the wizard Bigby in honour of his faithful hound Bigboy, who could use teleportation to fetch, snare errant cats, and generally harass postmen.
Apparently this is what happens to your handwear when you clock an angel in the chops.

items.name: Go To the Open Hand Temple Cellar
Bring the Sentient Amulet to Shirra Clarwen
items.description:
Handle the event as you wish, the outcomes produce different reward:

• Sentient Amulet - if you decide to defeat the monk.

• Sentient Amulet - if you decide to remove the curse. You will also obtain Tasha's Hideous Laughter .​

items.name: Sentient Amulet
items.description: Improved version of the amulet, if you refused the curse.

tooltip.lore: Sun-graced warmth radiates from both the pendant and its chain. Somehow, even a brief glance of it brings a smile to your face.
The dagger's twisting blade is designed to nick the flesh as many times as possible, allowing its paralytic poison to flow effortlessly into any unfortunate target.

items.name: Speak With Gyldo Angleiron the Smith

tooltip.lore: This blade is one of a few rare swords wielded by the Eucalyptus League, a group of fabulously wealthy courtesans who owned a great deal of Luskan's bazaars.
Sharp juts and knobbly nodules of bone stud this armour, as if something ancient sank its teeth deep into the leather and they got stuck there.
The weight of the interwoven steel rings is offset by clever elven craftsmanship - rustling against your undershirt, the mail feels like you're wearing a cloud.

It's a difficult life being a modern rogue - everyone expects the surprise attack. These gloves assist in making sure that the surprisee doesn't live long enough to decide whether they were surprised.
A gnomish adventurer once used this to outpace his enemies down a slope.
Tiny specks of blood are fused into the blade, as if the glaive is feeding on them.

items.name: Avoid Coming With Shadowheart
items.description: If Shadowheart abandoned the Shar avoid coming with her to gain access to his wares.

items.name: Dwarven Thrower
items.description: One of the best-throwing weapons if you are a Dwarf.

tooltip.lore: The first Dwarven Thrower was created unintentionally. The moment was transcribed in the annals of dwarfdom thusly: 'Ach, it doesn't work. Another design down the bloody drain. Now to... Does er... Does anyone else hear a sort of humming whistle? Growing louder?'

items.name: Hellrider Longbow
items.description: Excellent bow to be used as a stat stick for bonus initiative.

tooltip.lore: This longbow was forged from the remnants of Stanislav the Silver's shield, found near the wreckage of Castle Ravenloft some years ago.
In the steel, you can see small ice formations like bubbles in a bath, and even hear them pop with crickling crackles of frosted delight.

The charmers, seducers, philanderers, flirts, gallants, beguilers, ravishers, rogues, tempters, and tantalisers of the Feywild all share one common trait: they make you feel as if you are the only diamond among the drift.

Long ago, the young wizard Bigby noticed his friend Tenser spending a lot of time with his robe. One of nature's pranksters, Bigby sent his dog, Bigboy, to savage the garment. The ensuing struggle between canine and nude wizard was brutal, and eventually led to Tenser designing more durable robes like this one.
When this axe careens through the air toward an unfortunate head - the whistle of its spiritual counterpart is never far behind.
'Let your lips turn black as night/On this eve of frightful wights/Let your throat be filled with screams/Nearby looms the Raven Queen!' - A song of dark festivity
Every adventuring party has at least one 'chaotic element'. In the case of one such party, this was the infamous Scabby, a barbarous goblin who was trained by monks.

items.name: Decide the Fate of a newborn Mind Flayer

tooltip.lore: 'Fib now, and forever hold your teeth.' - Folk saying
Wielded by the indomitable Ser Lexia. Although her eagerness to destroy evil in all its forms was constant, she often took time to make little gifts for her friends.

items.name: Gandrel's Aspiration
items.description: It can be obtained if you didn't in Act 1.

tooltip.lore: When Gandrel was seven, he was frightened badly by a vampire spawn during a public burning. Seized by the flames, the creature didn't shrivel up. It swelled and burst like a squeezed leech. Brains splattered the boy's shoes, grey-pink and pulpy. He would never forget it.

items.name: Necklace of Elemental Augmentation
items.description: Excellent necklace for Cold Sorcerer Build .

tooltip.lore: The tourmaline in this necklace is almost uncomfortably hot, warmed by a heat all its own.
Armour designed for the aerodynamic leaping and striking and general screaming of your standard barbarian.
A pair of pixie wings were used to create this pretty blade - their delicate tracings can be seen all day and night, though they shine brightest by the light of a gibbous moon.
Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.
Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.
Reliable and trustworthy, you could not hope for more respectable company than this maul whilst traversing the forlorn places of the world.

'You drop and give me thirty you sumbitch; you think a bugbear's gonna roll over and let you rub his tummy? One two three four - keep going maggot! Stronger, better, tougher!' - Any Sword Coast Drill Sergeant, Invariably
For the right wearer, these supple doeskin gloves offer both comfort and protection - alongside a perfectly attuned sense of where one's fingers are, and what pocket they're picking.
Each blow landed while wearing these gloves produces the rumbling boom of a thousand demons' ravenous stomachs.

items.name: Find a Way Inside Wyrm's Crossing
Talk With Naaber Until He Has Nothing Else To Say

tooltip.lore: The interlocked bronze links of this necklace tinkle softly every time you move.
The garnet at the centrepiece of this ring is like an unblinking, malevolent eye.

items.name: Harpers Safehouse
items.description: If Jaheira has told you about the Harpers safehouse or she is with you, Entharl will let you in the Harpers Safehouse.

tooltip.lore: Clothing doesn't tend to have much personality. That comes down to how it's worn. Yet these gloves exude a confidence, an intangible aura of expertise.

items.name: Cloak of Displacement
items.description: One of the best cloaks in the game.

tooltip.lore: The fur is so blue as to be almost black. When mantled across your shoulders, the patterns on the cloak seem to shift and ripple.

This was created by a designer and an enchanter who were going through a messy divorce at the time. As such, the armour is filled with active-aggressive rage. It also makes you feel like you've wasted the last ten years picking up after a complete tit.
Cracking your knuckles in these gloves dislodges puffy drifts of snow from your fingers.

items.name: Garb of The Land and Sky
Harmonic Dueller
items.description: Excellent weapon for Spellsword Builds that use Dual-Wielding.

tooltip.lore: Unsheathed, the steel of this rapier shivers and thrums mellifluously in response to nearby surfaces - the motion brings to mind an actor struck with pre-show nerves.
Conducting a series of increasingly sick rituals - involving the ashes of a gnome and silkworms fed fat on the corpse of a dwarven cleric - the goblin shaman Kirruk Redrats created this deadly bow.
Donning this necklace causes the room to sway and tip - or maybe it was already swaying? Whatever, time for another pint.

items.name: Speak With Sorn and Num Orlith
Talk With Arfur
items.description:
Only available if you sided with the refugees at Arfur's House.

He can give you an Admission Pass to enter the Wyrm's Rock Fortress.

items.name: Speak With Naoise Nallinto
items.description: Located on the top floor of Sharess Caress.

items.name: Speak With Raphael
items.description: Located on the top floor of Sharess Caress.

items.name: Speak With Kith'rak Voss
items.description: If Voss survived Act 1 you can find him after speaking with Raphael near Valeria .

tooltip.lore: Heavy and grouted in the gore of countless centuries, this prototype for the contemporary githyanki silver sword was wielded during their rebellion against the mind flayers who enslaved them.

items.name: Talk With Dalyria and Pale Petras
items.description: Only possible if you do not have Daylight ​ effect active or party has not equiped Blood of Lathander .

items.name: Find and Investigate the Hidden Room
items.description: On the third floor there is a hidden room which can either be unlocked with the Flowery Key or DC 25 Sleight of Hand roll.

items.name: Convince Arkleia Oloril to Sell You Her Wares
items.description:
Notable items for sale:

• Arrow of Aberration Slaying

• Arrow of Dragon Slaying

• Arrow of Many Targets

• Arrow of Transposition

tooltip.lore: The air around this armour shimmers, oven-hot. From the seams of the armour you can hear a red-tinted rumble, like coals digesting in a furnace.

items.name: Speak With Guests at the First Floor
items.description: Before proceeding to the Audience Hall speak with the guests at the mess hall.

items.name: Speak With Enver Gortash at the Audience Hall
items.description:
It is better to not yet start a confrontation with Gortash as the battle will be very difficult to win.

I recommend starting a fight with Gortash after destroying the Steel Watch Foundry .

items.name: Defeat Enver Gortash
items.description: It is best to fight him after dealing with the Steel Watch .

items.name: Helldusk Boots
items.description: Excellent end-game boots.

tooltip.lore: No ore takes better to being forged than the infernal iron of Avernus, where the archdevil Zariel presides.

items.name: Servitor of the Black Hand Gloves

tooltip.lore: Covetous of Mystra's command of the Weave, Bane the Black Hand extends his grasp, each finger comprised of ten thousand fanatics, adjuncts of his evil will.

One can hardly be called a lord if they aren't dressed as such. A cursory glance at Gortash's finery would reveal intricate garb - a closer look would reveal a maddening attention to even the most minute details of the filigree.
A perfect system must be ruled by a keen eye - and any error foolish enough to rear its head afore the eye must be quickly, precisely, and totally eliminated. This crossbow can help with that.
Each step taken in Gortash's shoes is a reflection of the man himself: quick, sharp, and terrible.

items.name: Speak With Marvin the Unusual Skull
items.description: Only available if you get arrested and jailed. He will be in your cell and will offer to get you out of prison.

items.name: Save Counsellor Florrick from Wyrm's Rock Prison
Find the Hidden Passage to the Wyrmway
items.description: You will need to use shock damage on the two dragon heads. It will reveal a fake wall which you can cross.

items.name: Justice Trial
items.description: Dismiss the corrupt judge.

items.name: Insight Trial
items.description: Find out which assertion is wrong.

items.name: Strategy Trial
items.description: Find a way to win the lanceboard in two moves.

items.name: Courage Trial
items.description: Defend the torch bearer against waves of enemies.

items.name: Enter the Lair and Speak With Ansur
Balduran's Giantslayer
items.description: It's the best two-handed sword for good runs without Bhaalist Armour .

tooltip.lore: Wielded by Balduran, the founder of Baldur's Gate and friend to his guardian dragon, a great glittering wyrm called Ansur. Fellowship can be undone, though, as easily as you or I might unlace the strings of our shoes, and it was in a time of skullduggery and hardship that Balduran killed Ansur, carrying out the deed with this sword.

items.name: Helm of Balduran
items.description: The helmet works well with some other items, to activate their bonuses - Broodmother's Revenge , Hellrider's Pride , The Reviving Hands .

tooltip.lore: This helm, worn atop the pate of Balduran the Brave, still gleams as if with the untarnished splendour of his legacy. Baldur's Gate itself bears his name, and tales of his exploits have turned the heads and hearts of many.

items.name: Stop the Presses
items.description: Speak with Estra Stir.

items.name: Help the Hag Survivors
items.description: Starts by reading one of the many Anti-Hag Support Group posters.

items.name: Speak With People at the Training Grounds
Save Vanra
items.description: Speak with Lora inside the barracks.

items.name: Counting House Safe nº2 Key
items.description: Sold by Skittle.

items.name: Pray and Give an Offering to Your God
Gale's Audience With Mystra
items.description: Depending on where you are with his companion quest.

items.name: Loot Stormshore Tabernacle Basement
items.description: Basement contains offering chests and will get you cursed if you try to steal the items.

items.name: Amulet of The Devout
items.description: The best amulet for casters in general, but it is also extremely valuable on Cleric Builds .

tooltip.lore: Otto, a member of the powerful arcane organisation the Circle of Eight, had a penchant for amulets. So much so that they announced his arrival before he ever needed to.
Tyr is often known as the Maimed God - a descriptor he shares with most victims of this heavy warhammer.
Named in the style of Extremely Unimaginative Jimmy, the geographer and interior decorator who named the 'waterfall' and the 'fireplace'.
This garment accidentally fell into the communal steambaths during the All Saints Morning Retreat And Candlelit Guided Meditation.
Stitched into the lining is the phrase: 'Yes all right, all right, stop moaning and get up.'
The power of this item lies in belief, though not in any god. This item believes that it's very good at hitting things, so it is. Belief is quite powerful when it comes to violence.

items.name: Speak With the Bartender About a Murder
Rent the Rooms
items.description: You will get a new campsite. You will also be able to buy camp supplies from the Dumbwaiter.

items.name: Roveer's Storehouse
items.description: Clear the rats from the Elfsong Tavern Basement .

tooltip.lore: Created by a conglomerate of druids one moonlit night when all the stirring violins of insect, elms, and whispering winds consorted, and when wild beasts in need of taming prowled unseen.

items.name: Find the Entrance to Knights of the Shield Hideout
Warn Roveer About a Possible Assasination Plot
items.description: Only possible after completing Roveer's Storehouse

items.name: Obtain Harvard Willoughby the Sheep
items.description:
Completing certain actions will provide you with a unique Lvl 1 follower, here are the steps (in no particular order):

• Harvard Willoughby must be dead

• Bell in Open Hand Temple must be rung

• Bell in Lower City must be rung

After all of these conditions are met go to your Rivington campsite and you will find a Mysterious Egg behind the hen houses.

items.name: Investigate the Murder in the Elfsong Tavern
items.description: Located upstairs.

items.name: Hr'a'cknir Bracers
items.description: Available only if you have not been marked for death by Vlaakith.

tooltip.lore: The planar silver cladding of these forearm bracers allows the wielder to channel the latent astral energies floating in the Weave.

items.name: Psionic Ward Armour
items.description: Available only if you have not been marked for death by Vlaakith.

tooltip.lore: After all that was done to them at the illithids' caprice, it is little wonder that the gith would construct armaments fit for combating their psionic might.
When not atop their dragons, gith are not particularly air-worthy. This is probably for the best. The imposition of downward gravity tends to put most people in a mood.
The little tentacle engravings are a nice touch.
The taut violet leather is drier than snakeskin, and puckered at each knuckle with shallow mouth-shaped pits.
There's a graceful swarthiness to this sword. The inscription in the steel reads - 'My eye wanders far from home / To shores of distant Anchorome.'

items.name: Stop the Presses
items.description:
Important:

• You have to complete this quest before killing Lord Enver Gortash .

• After speaking with the editor-in-chief ( Ettvard Needle ) you will have until the next Long Rest to complete the quest.

Stop the publication of a negative article about the party.

Possible outcomes :

• Positive article: people around town recognize the party in a more positive light, attitude increases by 10 (including traders). Requires one of these article blocks:

• Make Big Money by Trading with Adventurers

• Adventurers, Our Best Hope For the City

• Celebrate the Brave Heroes Who Slew General Ketheric

• Neutral article: nothing changes. Require one of these article blocks:

• Cutest Cat You've Ever Seen

• Graverobbers' Spree

• Hear the Finest Shanties at the Blushing Mermaid

• Negative article: people around town recognize the party in a negative light, attitude decreases by 15 (including traders). Requires one of these article blocks:

• A Public Danger!

• Curse-Breakers or Curse-Makers?

• Wolves in Sheeps' Clothing

items.name: Warn Cora Highberry About an Assasination Plot
Loot Highberry's Home Basement
items.description: You can find Elixir of Universal Resistance , Potion of Flying , some random scrolls and gold.

items.name: Counting House Vault nº4 Key
items.description: Carried by Roger Highberry.

items.name: Speak With Tara on the Roof of Highberry's Home
items.description: Only available if you encountered her on the top of Open Hand Temple and on the roof of Devil's Fee . She will sell you Slinging Shoes .

tooltip.lore: These shoes were formerly enchanted to return to their owner's feet. This caused a great deal of trouble for owners, who, while wearing a slipper or a nice heeled number, kept being kicked in the ankles by jealous footwear.
Once wielded by a murderer, the weapon's handle retains an eerie, clammy warmth, as if Dolor's pre-slaughter sweat somehow crept into the leather - and lingers there still.
This well-tailored leather tunic is reinforced by geometric quilting.
These boots are well-maintained and clean, though their soles are thin from wear.
These gauntlets spark with unfinished, yet still potent devillish magic - indicative of a cancelled blacksmith's commission.
These boots are stamped with the marks of a cancelled commission. Despite their discarded fate, they still hum with complex infernal arcana.
Formerly the property of the royalty of Threskel, these gauntlets impart some of the prince of Threskel's legendary skill with weapons.
Great sheets of steel were hammered and bashed into something like a carapace, thick and dense - you can almost feel the hammer blows, flooded with rich magic.

''If I had a copper for all the adventurin folk I seen gutted or cut up, or clawed like they was made of wet paper, just cause they couldn't move quick enough when speed might have saved em... well, I'd be suckin good brandy from crystal rather than this fuckin swill.'' - Overheard in a tavern in Baldur's Gate.

Nothing stings the master blacksmith more than a cancelled commission - not even molten iron. This mail is engraved with the mark of a devillish forgemaster, as well as several curses stamped out in Infernal, and hums with diabolic magic.

items.name: Find a Companion for Derryth
items.description:
If Baelen Bonecloack did not survive in Underdark you can ask one of the local cats to stay with Derryth.

Located around Sorcerous Sundries .

items.name: Counting House Safe nº3 Key
items.description: Located in the basement of the Bonecloack's Apothecary, inside a heavy chest.

items.name: Armour of Agility
items.description: The best medium armor in the game.

tooltip.lore: Layered sheets of smelted, shaped, and smithed steel have been etched with a sweetly luminous gold. Its outward appearance is heavy. And yet it's so light when you lift it, like lifting a bag of shrunken dead leaves.
Created by a warmongering rakshasa and kept for a time under the careful watch of an astute noble named Lord de'Arnise, this flail is a tool of absolute destruction, and a versatile one at that.

items.name: The Dead Shot
items.description: Excellent bow that can be used as a stat stick to improve crit range.

tooltip.lore: When the archer Frederik Durlusk died, his only request was to be buried with his favourite bow. This is that bow.

items.name: Speak With Rolan
items.description: Only available if he survived Act 2.

items.name: Speak With Tolna Tome-Monger
items.description: Find out how to get The Annals of Karsus.

items.name: Choose the Correct Portal to Meet Lorroakan
items.description: The portals are located on the 2nd floor only one is correct. You will need to select the one portal which has the correct description of Nightsong.

items.name: Find the Entrance to the Sorcerous Vault
Armour of Landfall
items.description: Best caster robe if you have light armour proficiency.

tooltip.lore: Smelling of long-abandoned gardens grown wild with thorny roses, moss and hibiscus, this armour was worn by a powerful druid who spent so long from people that she forgot to miss them.

items.name: Birthright
items.description: Best charisma-based caster hat.

tooltip.lore: 'The thing that you poor sods simply cannot grasp is how effortless all this magical hootie-wootie castie-spellsie business is for me!' - A very stupid sorcerer at a wizard academy ball.
This quarterstaff is as solid as the bond between each member of the Harpers - even working in anonymity, they do so with a common and righteous cause.

These gloves were worn by the wizard Bucknard, who always maintained that speed was most important in spellcasting. This, because even if you royally bugger up the spell, you've got enough time to leg it away afterward.

items.name: Ring of Regeneration
items.description: The ring works well with some other items, to activate their bonuses - Broodmother's Revenge , Hellrider's Pride , The Reviving Hands .

tooltip.lore: This ring is, in effect, a tiny field medic that won't charge you 70 gold pieces for a fifteen-minute consultation.

items.name: Vest of Soul Rejuvenation
items.description: Top-tier Monk armor.

tooltip.lore: Mauls
Anything hit by this has the life expectancy of a glass hammer.
Merely holding the circlet makes your skin flush, filling you with determination and righteous fury.

items.name: Speak With Lorroakan
items.description: Decide if you want to help him.

items.name: High Security Vault nº1 Key
items.description: Located on the lower level of Ramazith's tower. You will need to jump down from the level where Lorroakan is located.

items.name: Markoheshkir
items.description:
The best caster weapon in the game.

Located on the lower level of Ramazith's tower. You will need to jump down from the level where Lorroakan is located.

tooltip.lore: Kereska, draconic goddess of arcane knowledge, imbues many chromatic dragons with their magic. This includes their exquisite and terrible breath, which has wrenched giants from their tall thrones, and destroyed civilisations of smallfolk.

items.name: Robe of the Weave
items.description:
Top-tier caster armor.

Located on the lower level of Ramazith's tower. You will need to jump down from the level where Lorroakan is located.

tooltip.lore: Earnest little Edith Cluckle was a small girl when she asked to be the great wizard Otiluke's apprentice. He scoffed at her, but was quickly abashed when Mystra herself endowed Edith with this very cloak.

Touching the adornments, it's clear that Lorroakan's short temper has flowed into his staff. It flows into your brain in shades of burgundy and carmine, bright as the storm-lanterns of Lorroakan's homeland.

Mantled in the sublime majesty of this cloak, you magically sense Lorroakan's complex feelings for his homeland. Merriment and comfort go hand in hand with a jilted sentiment around his lovers and his family. And the old friends he thought of, but would never see again.

items.name: Speak With People at Felogyr's Fireworks
Defeat the Cultists
items.description:
This will wrap up the Investigate the Suspicious Toys quest.

It might be easier to do this encounter if you have already dealt with the Steel Watch Foundry .

items.name: Loot Felogyr's Fireworks
items.description: Loot items on all the floors and don't forget to loot the Basement .

items.name: Find the Mystic Carrion's Servant
items.description: Strange Beggar Lumbar might know where the servant is.

items.name: Staff of Cherished Necromancy
items.description: One of the only weapons that perfectly fits the Necromancer theme.

tooltip.lore: Unlike their father, Thibault, Cherish Hollow was not afraid of zombies. Cherish was afraid of sharks, liquorice, wallpaper, Netherese artefacts, hats (large), the outdoors, the indoors, hats (small), and milk pails. Zombies were a bloody picnic.
This staple of any worthwhile exorcist burns with a pale flame that destroys the anchors of troublesome ghosts in need of banishment.

items.name: Armour of the Sporekeeper
items.description: One of the best Spore Druid armors for Haste Spores .

tooltip.lore: According to one story, this armour was enriched by the cascading spores of a myconid sovereign, its great fungal head shimmering and twinkling as it shook upon the material.

items.name: Hood of the Weave
items.description: Best caster headwear in the game

tooltip.lore: Worn by the wizard Tenser when he was inducted into the Circle of Eight. After the ceremony in which his fellow wizards Otiluke and Bigby played a trick on Tenser, the two pranksters found their party vodkas spiked with a spell that transformed their earlobes into cynical pixies.
Crafted from celestial feathers, bottled sunlight, and ink from the pages of sacred scripture, it's a wonder the maker didn't hang cloves of garlic from this robe, so anathema is it to the undead.

Slipping one's finger through this ring fills the senses with the waft of tombs, the urine-yellow smell of old bandages, images of dead spiders curled into husks, and the sensation of hot, slobbering breath at the nape of one's neck.
This breastplate is far tougher than it appears, taking blows from common weapons without suffering so much as a scratch.
A common and effective sword, carried by adventurers, thieves, and watchmen alike.

items.name: Speak With Dravo and Sally Flymm
Staff of Interruption
items.description: Received as a reward for completing Help the Hag Survivors .

tooltip.lore: This staff hums with a disruptive arcane energy that drives nearby spellcasters to distraction.

items.name: Avenge the Hag Survivors
items.description: Starts after completing Help the Hag Survivors .

tooltip.lore: Originally the kidney stone of an archfey, this object has been, ahem, passed down through the centuries.

items.name: Talk and Trade With Oliver Tefoco
items.description: Sells camp supplies

items.name: Counting House Safe nº7 Key
items.description: Can be looted from Oliver Tefoco.

items.name: Talk With Araj
items.description: Only available if she survives act 2 and party offered her their blood.

items.name: Murderous Cut
items.description: Carried by some Bhaal cultists.

tooltip.lore: Odd to think that dark gods like Bhaal were once mortal. One wonders if they ever thumbed a wagon ride, thought about the weather, or ate flavoured yoghurt.
Attached to this shield is a faded note. 'G, I've found the best defence against an arrow is to be the one behind the bow - but this shield also does the trick in a pinch. Yours, AA'
The hilt of this slender sword is wrapped with soft, black silk - the dark colour hiding any possible stains.

items.name: Speak With Society Of Brilliance Members
items.description: Available only if you have not given the Githyanki Egg to Lady Esther during Steal a Githyanki Egg quest.

items.name: Retrieve Omeluum
items.description: Starts here if you have not delivered the Githyanki Egg to Lady Esther.

items.name: Mind Flayer Parasite Specimen
items.description: Located on the second floor in the library.

tooltip.lore: Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.

items.name: Unflinching Protector Amulet
items.description: Available only if you have delivered the Githyanki Egg to Lady Esther in Act 1.

tooltip.lore: Touching this amulet's centrepiece, you are imbued with a vision of woven strands, and the strands coming apart and holding true and firm over your friends, like shields banded in a haze of golden light.

items.name: Investigate the Manor
items.description: If you saved Oskar Fevras in Act 1 the manor will be haunted and you will have to help Lady Jannath . If Oskar was not saved there will be an art exhibition.

tooltip.lore: Love is the sweetest bird that might migrate to the charming country of your heart.

items.name: After Death Do Us Part
items.description: Only obtainable if Oskar Fevras was rescued in Act 1.

tooltip.lore: Love is the last cruel leaf to fall in an autumn wide with sorrow.

items.name: Speak With Inhabitants at Elerrathin's Home

tooltip.lore: Quarterstaffs

Faded but still legible, the mark of a Kara-Turan forgemaster adorns the pommel of this exquisite sword. Light as a robin's wing, quick as gnomish wit, this weapon has unseamed many foes - and will continue to, in the correct hands.

items.name: Khalid's Gift
items.description: The best monk amulet with bonus +1 Wisdom.

tooltip.lore: A gift from Khalid for Jaheira.

items.name: Deal With Dolor
items.description: Available only if Dolor escaped during ambush in Highberry's Home / Wine Festival .

items.name: Buy Some Fancy Clothes
items.description: Available only if Figaro Pennygood is not killed by Dolor.

items.name: Gain Access to Cloister of Sombre Embrace
Handmaiden's Mace
items.description: An alternative stat stick to Club of Hill Giant Strength if you missed it.

tooltip.lore: This mace was designed by Lolth's assassins to fell heretics who dared turn their back on the Spider Queen. However, even the best-laid designs fall to nothingness when brought to bear against Shar's faithful.

Listening to the magical hum this robe emits dulls your senses, thoughts, feelings - everything. Listen too long, and you can almost hear the screaming chitters of a thousand spiders devoured by encroaching, hungry darkness. Almost.

items.name: Viconia's Walking Fortress
items.description: The best shield in the game.

tooltip.lore: 'Walking Fortress' is a defensive technique reliant on a shield of this calibre. It involves upsetting your opponent, who just wants to land a single blow.

items.name: Rescue Volo
items.description: Available only if Volo survives Act 1.

tooltip.lore: Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.
Little jellyfish-shaped pockets line the inside of this cloak.

Tales tell of a most wondrous fish, scales resplendent, an intricate jewel that shone beneath the sea. When it died, the Wavemother gifted its hide as a robe to her most devoted follower - and demanded she drown the sailors that killed her gem-bright fish.
The curve of this sickle evokes a pitiless crescent moon over turbulent seas.

items.name: Save the Hostages
items.description: There are multiple hostages held inside the Iron Throne.

items.name: Retrieve Omeluum
items.description: Only available if you have not give Lady Esther the Githyanki Egg.

tooltip.lore: Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.
The tabard adorning this perfectly kept plate denotes the status of its wearer - Marshal of the Flaming Fist and ultimate military authority in Baldur's Gate.

Cradling his newborn son awkwardly, the Duke's face pulled into a rictus of misery. His love Francesca smiled at him, briefly, and died. He stared at her until the boy cried, and he told the boy it would be all right, though he himself did not believe it.

items.name: Save the Gondians
items.description: If you want the gondians to survive you have to go to the Iron Throne first.

items.name: Steelwatcher Helmet
items.description: Important note: you have to do this before starting the fight with Steel Watcher Titan (Lv. 11-12) .

tooltip.lore: 'When I found Carixim, he turned to me, with all those poor people's souls crammed into him, and he... he grinned at me. He said, "I been expecting you, boy."' - Overheard from the smith Dammon

items.name: Craft the Hellfire Engine Crossbow
items.description: Important note: you have to do this before starting the fight with Steel Watcher Titan (Lv. 11-12) .

tooltip.lore: 'Trundling, mashing, mincing, crushing, grinding, blasting, biting! Hear their peeling thunder, their roaring flames, their clicking, whirring, metallic melody, and know that the hellfire engines of Avernus are on the move!' - Edict in Elturel
Good for a winkle in cases of mechanisms, locks, or some bugger who needs killing.

items.name: Gontr Mael
items.description: Important note: loot the weapon before continuing as later the weapon will not be accessible.

tooltip.lore: Gontr Mael blasted from the corpse of the Steel Watcher Titan: a bow formed from the boiling slag and weird whispering gyros inherent to the mechanical giant. It must have acted as the Titan's abdomen, exchanging muscle for immaculate steel.

items.name: Infernal Mace
items.description: Carried by Flaming Fists.

tooltip.lore: Mortician Kurwin was known to use his trusty Cauteriser on all sorts of interesting subjects. At first these were cadavers, then people who wouldn't be missed, and it was only after he targeted a well-liked tavern owner that he was ratted out to the law.
The base of this polearm's shaft is splintered, likely having borne the weight of a rider plucked from their mount.

items.name: Explore the Lower City Graveyard
items.description:
Explore these locations at the graveyard:

• Ancient Mausoleum

• Durinbold Mausoleum

• Mortuary

• Hhune Mausoleum

• G orion Mausoleum

tooltip.lore: For some paladins, the iron-clad grip that an oath holds can be too much to bear. When these champions fail and falter, their hands that would guide turn cold, hard, and piercing.

Clad on the desiccated feet of a death knight, these boots have walked a long and terrible path. They strode through the tattered remains of their owner's oath. With every heavy footstep, they have sounded pure fear into the hearts of men.

To break one's oath is a dangerous game - it marks you as a faithless cur, an exile without a cause. This armour is tempered by an age of rocks, blades, and persecution turned against it - and is buffed by the immutable, unbindable will of its wearer.
Formerly wielded by one who forsook all ties of friendship and kindness, this sword is rife with deranged energies. It seems to transmit feelings of contentment when carving through bones.
The sword of Sarevok, brother of Gorion's Ward and child of the evil god Bhaal. Though much of its power died with him, the sword is still formidable, and glows with a malevolent light.

The last sight Sarevok witnessed as a mortal was his sibling's knife. This duel, the first between Bhaalspawn, is worshipped still by the cult of Bhaal, who now believe that no act is more sacred than fratricide between spawn before the altar of their Dread Lord.
Where once this shield would protect the rallying forces of good - now it pushes them back, crushing them against their allies until they pop.

items.name: Decide Fate of the Bhaal Cultist's Prisoner

tooltip.lore: When he was killed, Bhaal's blight upon the world was not destroyed. For the many people upon whom he had sired his spawn slithered out of tainted wombs, and with their arrival Bhaal's malcontent returned with force.

items.name: Bhaalist Armour
items.description: The best light armor in the game.

tooltip.lore: Light Armour

items.name: Craterflesh Gloves
items.description: Excellent gloves if you are doing an Eldritch Blast spam build or Sorlock . They are bugged and create another instance of the ray.

tooltip.lore: Indents on the fingertips of these gloves are ringed with tiny meteorites, lined in a shimmering purple light rather than the rich red of astral flame.
Once wielded by a murderer, the weapon's handle retains an eerie, clammy warmth, as if Dolor's pre-slaughter sweat somehow crept into the leather - and lingers there still.
A web of veins runs the length of the blade - it has become a slender iron mortuary for all the innocents it has tasted.
The previous owner was going to call this sword Barney, but he got such a bollocking from his friends that he named it Fleshrender instead, and sulked for a whole tenday afterward.
A pulse of magic shoots through your hands whenever you touch this axe.
Pliable yew and a hearty bowstring ensure this shortbow fires straight and true.

items.name: Explore the Bulshing Maid Tavern and Basement
items.description: If you allowed The Hag in Act 1 to keep Mayrina's baby the tavern will not have much events, but if you killed or didn't allow Mayrina to give away her baby there will be a few interesting events and encounters.

tooltip.lore: Hanging suspended in murky slime, the living tadpole looks like some hideous cousin to the slug, only gorged and slick with remnants of brain fluid.
Shiver me timbers, this be a devilishly nasty blade.

items.name: Ring of Feywild Sparks
items.description: The best ring for casters. It has the unlisted effect of +1 Spell Save DC

tooltip.lore: This otherwise unassuming ring ties a spellcaster indelibly to the sporadic arcana of the Faerie realm.
A menacing cackle emanates from this twisted charm. It whispers to bite, to maim, to kill...

items.name: Amulet of Windrider
items.description: Received as a reward for saving Vanra.

tooltip.lore: Heavily embossed with filigree, this necklace reflects light in all directions.

items.name: Duellist's Prerogative
items.description: Received as a reward for completing Save Vanra .

tooltip.lore: A blade gifted to Lora by her own mother, Lenore the Fleet. When challenged to duels, Lenore's opponents assumed she had received this nickname from some naval exploit. They found out how wrong they were when her blade blurred faster than the eye could follow.

items.name: Obtain a Bank Pass
items.description:
The bank pass can be acquired the following ways:

• Paying a hefty sum of gold

• Using Detect Thoughts ​ or passing an Insight check on Head Clerk Meadhoney

• Passing Persuassion check.

• Stealing it from the upstairs Head Clerks office

items.name: Amulet of Restoration
items.description: Second amulet. The previous one is obtained in Act 1.

tooltip.lore: The gemstone centrepiece of the amulet was long ago mottled and dyed by the tears of a martyr.

items.name: Loot the Safes at the Counting House Basement
items.description: To access the safes you will need to bypass the iron doors by using Gaseous Form ​, Shapechanger ​, Misty Step ​ or Arrow of Transposition .

items.name: Loot the Vaults at the Counting House
items.description: To access the vaults you will either have to jump over the teleportation glyphs or use a ranged attack on the two buttons in the room to disable them.

items.name: Loot the High Security Vaults at the Counting House
Get Access to the High Security Vaults
Return Rakath's Gold
items.description: Only available if Rakath survived the encounter.

items.name: Elegant Studded Leather
items.description: Received as a reward for completing Return Rakath's Gold or alternatively can be stolen from the High Security Vault nº9.

tooltip.lore: After she returned from her confinement at the hands of the demon prince Graz'zt, the Goddess of prosperity, Waukeen, appointed a champion, who donned this studded leather and took up arms against those who had schismed and corrupted her church in the interim.

The charmers, seducers, philanderers, flirts, gallants, beguilers, ravishers, rogues, tempters, and tantalisers of the Feywild all share one common trait: they make you feel as if you are the only diamond among the drift.
The corner of modern fashion that this hat currently occupies might well be called Optimistic.

Umberlee, the goddess of the sea, is deeply capricious, intelligent, and not nearly as wet around the edges as her domain might suggest. She often demands sacrificial tribute from sailors, such as their ships, their cargo, and, while she's at it, them.

Duke Ravengard's father was the sort of man who works with his hands, and communicates in grunts. In his heart his son vowed to do better. But when Wyll was born, Ravengard felt a strange gravity that drew him away from his son.
In the palm of the left glove is a glossy scarlet solution that coats Thieves' Tools, making them more reliable. In the right, arcane symbols wax and wane.
When on the wrong side of a few sherries, the powerful wizards Bigby and Tenser would sometimes see how many spells they could fit into amulets like this one before they burst.

items.name: The Long Arm of the Gur

tooltip.lore: HeavyCrossbows

The texture of magic granted by a warlock's patron is largely dependent upon the patron themselves - for example, your standard eldritch patron's probably going to work some eyeball-mottled tentacles in there somewhere.

items.name: Cloak of The Weave
items.description: The best cloak for casters in the game.

tooltip.lore: Part of an outfit worn by the wizard Tenser when he was inducted into the organisation of eminent wizards called the Circle of Eight. Otiluke and Bigby, who were fellow wizards and absolute devils for amusing themselves, enchanted the medallion that was given to Tenser so that it would explode into flatulent terriers when he touched it to his brow.
Designed by an arcane smith with half-vision. He made these gloves to make sure his spells hit their target.

items.name: Gibus of the Worshipful Servant
items.description: Comes with an Advantage on Concentration Saving Throws. This makes it easy to sustain spell effects even if you get hit. Use it on support character.

tooltip.lore: Corroded by Sceleritas' insouciant perversion, this little gibus hat was worn by the Butler before he sank throat-deep into Bhaal's clutches. Once, it was beyond besmirchment. Once, it was lovely.
Salvaged from the beard of a dead infernal leviathan, whose facial hair was formed from the weapons of an army it had once vanquished.

'Trust is the most fundamental part of wild-shaping. Not trusting others, but trusting yourself. Because the assumption of an animalistic form comes part and parcel with risk - that you'll like being a beast. Like it too much. You've got to trust yourself to the extent that you're comfortable courting that danger. Trust yourself the way you trust a marital partner with your heart.' - Mac Tíre, Moon Druid
Sorcerers operate on a first-come, first-blasted basis.

items.name: Get Inside and Investigate Helsik's Private Quarters
Complete the Ritual to Access House of Hope
items.description:
All required materials are sold by Helsik .

When you have all the ingredients you will need place them in this order (clockwise):

• Skull - closest to the altar

• Coin of Mammon

• Skip

• Diamond

• Incense

Place infernal marble in the center.

tooltip.lore: Formerly worn by Autumnal Baskin, a professional meditator turned bare-knuckle champion, who gained enlightenment via two methods. The first was giving up her addictive reliance on secluded caves and lonely mountaintops, and getting a bit more sociable. This involved many new skirts. Most of them lavender.

items.name: Hat of the Sharp Caster
items.description: There might be a bug that the hat is not in Tara's inventory to solve this bug read the wiki article .

tooltip.lore: All you need now is a holster for your wand, tobacco to chew, and some sort of pack animal to ride, and you'll look like a complete and utter bingus.

items.name: Speak With the Archivist
items.description: He can provide you with the Boudoir Invitation where you can use the Restoration Faucet to long rest (doesn't restore quick rest).

items.name: Amulet of Greater Health
items.description: Excellent amulet that allows you to totally disregard the Constitution and spend ability points elsewhere.

tooltip.lore: Worn by Lumi, a cleric beholden to twilight, who sheltered her fellow adventurers as a bantam shelters her chicks.
Your basic hill giant is just a landslide with a digestive system.
Every contract a devil makes has some form of insurance - this one is cast in infernal iron and was forged in deep, ruinous hellfire.

items.name: Restoration Faucet
items.description: Don't forget to use as often as you can.

items.name: Speak With Haarlep
items.description: You can acquire the key to unlock the safe to obtain Raphael's Notes either peacefully or by defeating him.

items.name: Helldusk Gloves
items.description: One of the best gloves both for martials and casters.

tooltip.lore: What precisely confers eternal punishment, reward, absolution? Perhaps it's not just belief in a god. Perhaps the deciding factor is whether the gods believe in you.

items.name: Soul Coin
items.description: Inside Raphael's safe.

items.name: Retrieve Mol's Contract
items.description: You can bring it and show it to Mol

items.name: Helldusk Helmet
items.description: One of the top helmets in the game. Gives plenty of bonuses and unique effects.

tooltip.lore: It is said that Carixim, the fiendish titan whose flesh squirms with twenty-thousand trapped souls, forged the pieces of this armour set.

items.name: Staff of Spell Power
items.description: Excellent staff, giving a free cast each Long rest.

tooltip.lore: A near-perfect relic that is only blemished by the collected palm-sweat of various archmagi from across the Realms.
A paralysing eyestalk harvested from a beholder was boiled in a broth, and poured down the throat of a dwarven ringsmith. Not one fortnight thence, he crafted this ring.

items.name: Helldusk Armour
items.description: The only heavy armor in the game that does not require proficiency. Excellent piece of gear when you need damage reduction.

tooltip.lore: Carixim, soul-stuffed forgemaster of hellish Avernus, hammers away ceaselessly at new creations. Questions of morality and destiny do not concern him. Conscience is for his betters. He works with cheer, his swollen skin moaning soothingly.

items.name: Gloves of Soul Catching
items.description: The best gloves for Monk Builds .

tooltip.lore: Formerly worn by Autumnal Baskin, a professional meditator turned bare-knuckle champion, who gained enlightenment via two methods. The second was getting a lot of fighting acumen. Advanced cosmic harmony is a nose. Sometimes you've got to break it a few times to get the best of things.
At the barest touch, you feel a surge of exuberance and stamina - like you could take on anyone and anything.

items.name: Deal With Godey
items.description: You will acquire the Szarr Family Ring which is required to progress further.

items.name: Enter the Ballroom and Defeat the Occupants
Counting House Safe nº1 Key
items.description: Inside an opulent chest.

items.name: Secret Puzzle Room
items.description: There is a hidden hatch under the opulent chest. The hatch can be unlocked with a silver key from the attic.

items.name: Speak With the Previous Vampire Lord

tooltip.lore: After the blade had sliced Kas the Bloody-Handed's throat, it was chosen by Pelor, God of the sun, to do righteous work against the undead.

items.name: Speak With Sebastian
items.description: If you bring Astarion, you will get unique dialogue.

items.name: Kill Cazador Szarr
items.description:
You will have to decide what to do next after defeating Cazador:

• If you have not killed a single vampire that is part of the Black Mass Ritual:

• Allow Astarion to finish the ritual

• Convince Astarion to kill Cazador and to stop the ritual.

• Refuse to help Astarion - he will destroy the Woe and all the vampire spawn will be left to rot. Also Astarion leaves the party.

• Interupting the ritual - it is possible to interrupt the ritual mid-way. Consequences are same as above.

• Not bringing Astarion makes him furious but if you have good approval from him, he won't leave the party.

• If you killed one or more vampires that are part of the Black Mass Ritual, Astarion will finish off Cazador without any consequences to your approval.

items.name: Rhapsody
items.description: One of the best damage stat sticks in the games, usable on most builds

tooltip.lore: Cazador's love of poetry arose after he read one on the naked stomach of a dead child in his homeland. The child was hung from the lowest branch of a tree. Cazador read the poem, and looked at the child, and he knew that here was the artform for him.
This ring carries the symbol of the Szarr family and has an inscription in some crusty old language.

The gentle tap-tap-tap of a staff on stone sparked terror for all in Cazador's palace. It signalled an approaching storm, and all they could do was shrink into the background and pray its wrath would not fall on them.

items.name: Speak With Nine-Fingers
items.description: You can bring Jaheira along for some unique dialogue.

tooltip.lore: Long ago, Kushigo was a warrior. Bedecked in enormous, stag-antlered plate, his arrival spelled doom for the enemy. This flimsy cloth speaks nothing to that legacy.
Rough and coarse, the cloak's materials soften to silk when immersed in shadow.
With every rustle of this cloak, small moths like windblown embers appear around you - they flutter for a moment, then fade into inconsequence.

items.name: Combine Sarin's Skull With Skeleton
items.description: Bring Sarin's Skull to Sarin's Skeleton and combine them.

tooltip.lore: Imbued with a little luck after the item was pressed to the pursed lips of a saint of the goddess Tymora. Inscribed roughly and inexpertly on the underside: 'Lady Luck, don't you piss in my porridge oats!'

items.name: Defeat Stone Lord and the Cultists
items.description: Note: if you do not kill Stone Lord (only knock him out) it is possible to free him from the Absolute's control.

items.name: Bonespike Gloves
items.description: Imporant: kill and loot Strangler Luke right away as it is the only opportunity to acquire the gloves.

tooltip.lore: These gloves were soaked in the marrow of a Dragon Turtle, its bones broken and their fluid drained in sloppy chunks into vats.
This armour was designed by lunar-obsessed Cuthbertina, a gnomish arcane smith who now lives peacefully on a mountain plateau with her magical forge.
The tibia of a storm giant was ground and chiselled and reworked till it became this helm. The wearer can actually smell summer tempest clouds like maroon-dark dread while wearing the helm.
Concussive energy thumps like a mighty heartbeat from this pike.
These gloves were soaked in the marrow of a Dragon Turtle, its bones broken and their fluid drained in sloppy chunks into vats.

items.name: Crimson Mischief
items.description: One of the best weapons for Dual Wield builds.

tooltip.lore: When she was a girl, she toyed with nastiness and sadism. Children often grow out of this; they get hobbies. For Orin, that taste for cruelty fermented, and in a way she did find hobbies. Ones that screamed.
Hard, fleshy leather has been derived from a countless slew of victims to form this armour for Bhaal's fetid chosen.

The Lord of Murder wasn't always a lord. He was once just a petty killer. When Bhaal first drew blood in a Baldurian back-alley, it dropped to the ground and lingered. When he ascended, this dried blood-drop grew, warped - and became sharp.

items.name: Speak With Kith'rak Voss
items.description: Only available if he survived.

items.name: Stock Up On Supplies
items.description: Stock up and get ready for the final battles.

items.name: Admire the View From the Watchtower
items.description: Theres one Winged Horror (Lv. 3) in the tower.

items.name: Restoration Pod
items.description: Important: One time use only!

items.name: Defeat the Absolutists in the Courtyard
High Hall Encounters
items.description: It won't be as difficult as in the courtyard but you have to watch out for Nautiloid barrages. You can easily loose party members by the blast radius and being thrown out of the building.

items.name: Defeat the Netherbrain
items.description: The final battle
